name: npm-helper
description: NPM and Node.js package management, project configuration, and dependency troubleshooting.
capabilities: Provides npm, yarn, and pnpm command examples and comparisons
Explains semantic versioning and dependency management
Includes security auditing and vulnerability fixing instructions
Contains troubleshooting guides for common package issues
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation-based skill containing only markdown instructions and examples for NPM package management. The static analyzer flagged 174 pattern-based findings, but all are false positives triggered by documentation examples (bash commands, config file references, URLs). No executable code exists. The skill provides guidance only and cannot execute commands or access credentials.
valueStatement: Managing Node.js dependencies can be confusing with multiple package managers and version conflicts. This skill provides expert guidance for npm, yarn, and pnpm with practical examples and troubleshooting solutions.
limitations: Does not execute package manager commands directly
Requires user to run commands in their terminal
Does not access or modify user's project files

useCases.title: Learn Package Management Basics
useCases.description: Understand the differences between npm, yarn, and pnpm with practical examples for everyday tasks.

useCases.title: Optimize CI/CD Package Installation
useCases.description: Implement best practices for fast, reliable dependency installation in production environments.

useCases.title: Standardize Project Setup
useCases.description: Create consistent project structures and dependency management across your development team.

promptTemplates.title: Package Manager Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare npm, yarn, and pnpm for a React project that needs fast installation and workspace support. Which should I choose and why?

promptTemplates.title: Dependency Conflict Resolution
promptTemplates.prompt: I have peer dependency warnings for React when installing packages. Show me how to fix these using npm overrides.

promptTemplates.title: Security Audit Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a step-by-step workflow for auditing and fixing security vulnerabilities in my Node.js project using npm audit.

promptTemplates.title: Monorepo Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me migrate three separate Node.js projects into a single monorepo using npm workspaces with shared dependencies.

bestPractices: Always commit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml) to version control
Use npm ci in CI/CD pipelines for faster and more reliable installations
Run npm audit regularly and fix vulnerabilities before they become critical
Pin Node.js version using .nvmrc to ensure consistency across environments
antiPatterns: Never use sudo with npm install -g as it creates security vulnerabilities
Do not ignore peer dependency warnings as they indicate potential compatibility issues
Avoid committing the node_modules folder as it bloats your repository and causes conflicts

faq.question: Which package manager should I use for a new project?
faq.answer: For most projects, npm works great. Use pnpm for faster installs and disk efficiency, or Yarn for specific features like Plug'n'Play.

faq.question: What is the difference between npm install and npm ci?
faq.answer: npm install updates dependencies and lock file. npm ci does a clean install from the lock file, making it faster and more reliable for CI/CD.

faq.question: How do I migrate from npm to pnpm?
faq.answer: Install pnpm globally, then run 'pnpm import' to convert package-lock.json, or delete it and run 'pnpm install' for a fresh start.

faq.question: Is it safe to use npm audit fix --force?
faq.answer: Use with caution. It may install breaking changes. Review changes manually and test thoroughly before committing.

faq.question: Why am I getting peer dependency warnings?
faq.answer: Peer dependencies are not automatically installed. Install matching versions manually or use --legacy-peer-deps as a last resort.

faq.question: How do I speed up npm install in CI/CD?
faq.answer: Use npm ci, enable caching for ~/.npm, use --prefer-offline flag, and consider switching to pnpm for even faster performance.
